question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
question_tr: Cümlenin fiili için aşağıdakilerden hangisi doğrudur?
explanation_en: The verb "lebt" is the third-person singular of "leben"
explanation_tr: "Lebt" fiili, "leben" fiilinin üçüncü tekil şahıs hâlidir

sentence_de: In Deutschland leben etwa 3 Millionen Türken
translation_en: In Germany, approximately 3 million Turks live
question_en: Which one is the subject of the sentence?
explanation_en: The subject does the action, not always at the beginning. 'leben' (live) agrees with '3 Millionen Türken' (they)
explanation_tr: Eylemi yapan öznedir, cümlenin başında olmak zorunda değildir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Haare"?
explanation_en: "Haare“ is used in plural by nature when referring to the entire head of hair.
explanation_tr: Saç genellikle çoğul hâlde kullanılır

question_en: Which is correct for the verb of the sentence?
question_tr: Cümlenin fiili için aşağıdakilerden hangisi doğrudur?
explanation_en: The verb changes for each subject: 'ich habe', 'du hast', 'er/sie/es hat'
explanation_tr: Fiil her şahsa göre değişir: 'ich habe', 'du hast', 'er/sie/es hat'

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Frau"?
explanation_en: "Frau" is a feminine word, so it always uses the article "die" or "diese"
explanation_tr: "Frau" dişil olduğundan, her zaman "die" veya "diese" edatını kullanır

question_en: What is the case of the sentence?
explanation_en: The verb 'bist' (sein) always uses the nominative case for the word after it
explanation_tr: bist' (sein) fiili, kendinden sonra gelen ismi her zaman yalın hâlde tutar

translation_en: She is not a nice person
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Mensch"?
explanation_en: Mensch' is always a masculine noun regardless of biological sex; therefore, the article and adjective must match its grammatical gender even when describing a woman
explanation_tr: ‘Mensch’ biyolojik cinsiyetten bağımsız olarak her zaman erildir; bu nedenle, bir kadını tanımlarken bile ismin dilbilgisel cinsiyetine uyulmalıdır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "seine"?
explanation_en: The verb 'nimmt' needs an accusative object. 'Medizin' is feminine, 'sein' takes the '-e' ending to agree with the feminine accusative case
explanation_tr: “nimmt” fiili bir Akuzatif gerektirir (birşeyi almak). “Medizin” dişil olduğu için, “sein” dişil Akuzatif hâle uyum sağlamak için “-e” takısını alır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: Menschen' is the plural form of 'Mensch' and the verb 'müssen' is conjugated for a plural subject
explanation_tr: Menschen' kelimesi çoğul hâldedir ve fiil 'müssen' de bu çoğul özneye göre çekimlenmiştir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Kopfschmerzen"?
explanation_en: For compound nouns like 'Kopfschmerzen', the last word 'Schmerzen' decides the gender. 'Schmerzen' makes the word pllural
explanation_tr: Kopfschmerzen' gibi bileşik isimlerde, son kelime 'Schmerzen' cinsiyeti belirler. 'Schmerzen' çoğul olduğu için kelime çoğuldur

question_en: What case is used in the sentence?
explanation_en: With the verb “ist” (to be), the subject and what follows it both stay in the nominative
explanation_tr: “Ist” fiili kullanıldığında, özne ve onu takip eden kısım nominativ olur

translation_en: You should drink plenty of water
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The modal verb 'sollst' is in position 2. The main verb 'trinken' goes to the end. This is the 'modal verb' pattern
explanation_tr: sollst' modal fiili 2. sıradadır. 'trinken' asıl fiili mastar hâlinde sona gider. Bu, 'modal fiil' kalıbıdır

translation_en: I'm going to the doctor today
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Arzt"?
explanation_en: Arzt' is a masculine noun. The article 'der' becomes 'dem' after 'zu'
explanation_tr: Arzt' eril bir isimdir. 'der' artikeli 'zu' edatından sonra 'dem' olur

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: The subject ‘die Kinder’ uses ‘die’ because it is a plural noun. All plural nouns take ‘die’ as their article. ‘die’ could also be feminine singular, but in that case the verb would be ‘wäscht’
explanation_tr: Özne ‘die Kinder’ çoğul bir isim olduğu için ‘die’ artikeli kullanılır. Tüm çoğul isimler artikel olarak ‘die’ alır. ‘die’ aynı zamanda dişil tekil de olabilir, ancak o durumda fiil ‘wäscht’ olurdu

sentence_de: Er sieht heute leider nicht gut aus
translation_en: He doesn't look well today, unfortunately
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'aussehen' splits: 'sieht' stays in second position, and the prefix 'aus' goes to the end of the sentence. This is the pattern for separable verbs
explanation_tr: aussehen' fiili ikiye ayrılır: 'sieht' ikinci pozisyonda kalır, 'aus' öneki cümlenin sonuna gider. Bu ayrılabilir fiillerin kalıbıdır

sentence_de: Meine Großmutter ist schon achtzig Jahre alt
translation_en: My grandmother is eighty years old
question_en: What case is used in the sentence?
explanation_en: Subjects are always in the nominative case
explanation_tr: Özneler her zaman yalın hâldedir (Nominatif)

question_en: What is the case of the sentence?
explanation_en: The word 'für' always uses the Akkusative case for the noun that follows it
explanation_tr: für' kelimesi kendisinden sonraki ismi her zaman Belirtme hâli (Akuzatif) yapar

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "mich"?
explanation_en: The word "für" always makes the word after it accusative
explanation_tr: "für" edatı kendinden sonra gelen kelimeyi her zaman akuzatif yapar

translation_en: Why are you telling it to her?
question_en: Which statement about “ihr” is correct?
explanation_en: The verb 'sagen' needs a dative object (to whom), so 'ihr' is in the dative case
explanation_tr: Dativ hâli Türkçede, yönelme (-e), bulunma (-de) ve ayrılma (-den) hâllerine karşılık gelir. Cümlede 'Sagen' fiili kime sorusuna cevap veren bir dativ nesne ister

translation_en: I am chatting with my friends
question_en: Which statement about “mit” is correct?
explanation_en: The word 'mit' shows who you are with or what you are doing something with, so it always uses the dative case
explanation_tr: mit' kiminle veya neyle birlikte olduğunu gösterir, bu yüzden hep Dativ gerektirir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Wörterbuch"?
explanation_en: When two words combine to form a new noun, its article is determined by the last word
explanation_tr: İki kelime birleştiğinde, oluşan kelimenin artikeli sondaki kelimeye göre belirlenir

translation_en: Can you give it to us?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "uns"?
explanation_en: The verb 'geben' takes a dative object (to whom) — so 'wir' changes to 'uns'
explanation_tr: geben' fiili 'kime' sorusuna cevap istediği için yönelme hâli (Dativ) alır. Bu yüzden 'wir' zamiri 'uns' olmuştur

sentence_de: Können Sie das noch einmal wiederholen?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The modal verb 'können' changes its form and stays at the beginning. The main verb 'wiederholen' stays in its original form at the end of the sentence
explanation_tr: können' bir modal fiildir, özneye göre çekilir ve başta durur. Ana fiil 'wiederholen' ise cümlenin sonunda, yalın hâlinde kalır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'anrufen' splits: 'ruft' stays with 'Er', and 'an' goes to the end of the sentence. Other separable prefixes include 'an-', 'auf-', 'ein-', 'aus-', 'mit-', and 'vor-'
explanation_tr: ‘anrufen’ fiili ayrılabilir: ‘ruft’ özne ile kalır ve ‘an’ cümlenin sonuna gider. Diğer ayrılabilir ön ekler şunlardır: ‘an-’, ‘auf-’, ‘ein-’, ‘aus-’, ‘mit-’ ve ‘vor-’

sentence_de: Ich verstehe diesen Satz leider nicht
translation_en: I'm afraid I don't understand this sentence
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "nicht"?
explanation_en: The 'nicht' negates the verb 'verstehe', 'don't understand'. 'kein' is used when you negate a noun
explanation_tr: nicht' 'verstehe' fiilini olumsuz yapar. İsimleri olumsuz yapmak için 'kein' kullanılır

sentence_de: Wir sprechen im Kurs nur Deutsch
translation_en: We only speak German in the course
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Kurs"?
explanation_en: The word 'im' is a short form of 'in dem'. 'dem' shows that 'Kurs' is masculine and in the dative case
explanation_tr: im' kelimesi 'in dem'in kısa hâlidir. 'dem', 'Kurs'un eril ve dativ hâlinde olduğunu gösterir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: darf' is a modal verb that conjugates for 'ich'. It works with the main verb 'fragen' at the end of the sentence. The pattern is 'Modal verb + ... + main verb (infinitive)'
explanation_tr: darf', 'ich' için çekimlenen bir modal fiildir. Cümlenin sonundaki 'fragen' yalın hâlde kullanılır. Kalıp 'Modal fiil + ... + ana fiil (yalın hâl)' şeklindedir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The 'Sprechen Sie!' pattern is an imperative, used for polite commands
explanation_tr: Sprechen Sie!' kalıbı, kibar bir emir için kullanılan emir kipidir

translation_en: How do you spell your last name?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Nachnamen"?
explanation_en: Nachname' is a masculine noun because in compound words, the last word determines the gender
explanation_tr: Nachname' bir eril bir isimdir. Birleşik kelimelerde sondaki kelime ölçüt alınır

sentence_de: Ich antworte dir morgen auf deine Frage
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: antworten' adds standard endings like '-e' for 'ich' and does not change its main vowel, so it is regular conjugation
explanation_tr: antworten' fiili 'ich' için '-e' gibi standart ekler alır ve ünlü harfini değiştirmez; düzenli fiildir

sentence_de: Was sagst du zu dieser Idee?
translation_en: What do you think of this idea?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Idee"?
explanation_en: zu' makes the following words in the dative case. In the dative, 'die' changes to 'der'. Here, the reason 'diese' takes an '-r' at the end is that 'Idee' is feminine and in the dative case
explanation_tr: ‘zu’ kendinden sonraki kelimeleri dativ yapar. Dativ hâlinde ‘die’ ‘der’ olur. Burada ‘diese’ sonuna -r alır çünkü ‘Idee’ dişil ve dativ hâlindedir

sentence_de: Sie hat mir gestern eine Nachricht geschickt
translation_en: She sent me a message yesterday
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The 'hat... ge-t' pattern shows an action that happened in the past
explanation_tr: hat... ge-t' kalıbı, geçmişte olan bir eylemi gösterir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Fahrrad"?
explanation_en: Fahrrad' is in accusative. 'neu' takes the '-es' ending to identify the neuter gender of the noun, the article 'ein' remains in its base form
explanation_tr: “Fahrrad” Akuzatif hâldedir. “neu” sıfatı, ismin nötr (das) olduğunu göstermek için “-es” takısını alır, “ein” artikeli ise yalın hâlinde kalır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Ihre"?
explanation_en: Because it is capitalized, it refers to formal 'Sie'. Because of 'Bücher' is plural and Nominative, 'Ihre' is also plural and Nominative
explanation_tr: Büyük harfle yazıldığı için resmî 'Siz' demektir. 'Bücher' çoğul ve Yalın hâl (Nominatif) olduğu için 'Ihre' de ona uygun çekimlenip sonuna '-e' almıştır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "zum"?
explanation_en: The preposition "zu" requires the dative case. Since "Essen" is a neuter noun (das Essen), "das" becomes "dem" in dative, so "zu + dem" contracts to "zum"
explanation_tr: “zu” edatı yönelme hâli (Dativ) gerektirir. “Essen” nötr bir isim (das Essen) olduğu için Dativ hâlde “dem” kullanılır ve “zu + dem” “zum” olarak birleşir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'abschließen' is a separable verb where the prefix 'ab-' moves to the end of the sentence, and other common separable prefixes; 'auf-', 'an-', 'mit-', 'aus-', and 'ein-'
explanation_tr: ‘abschließen’ ayrılabilen bir fiildir; ‘ab-’ öneki cümlenin sonuna gider. Diğer yaygın ayrılabilen önekler: ‘auf-’, ‘an-’, ‘mit-’, ‘aus-’ ve ‘ein-’

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
question_tr: Cümlenin fiili için aşağıdakilerden hangisi doğrudur?
explanation_en: The verb 'aufstehen' consists of separable prefix 'auf-' and 'stehen'
explanation_tr: ‘aufstehen’ fiili, ayrılabilen önek ‘auf-’ ve ‘stehen’ fiilinden oluşur

sentence_de: Sie möchte ein Zimmer mit Balkon
translation_en: She would like a room with a balcony.
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "sie"?
explanation_en: Because the verb 'möchte' is conjugated for third-person singular (she), 'sie' is third-person singular. If it were 'möchten', it would be third-person plural
explanation_tr: Fiil 'möchte' üçüncü tekil şahıs (dişil) için çekimlendiği için 'sie' dişil tekil şahıstır. 'möchten' olsaydı üçüncü çoğul şahıs olurdu

translation_en: They live on the first floor
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "im"?
explanation_en: Stock' is masculine. Because 'wohnen' indicates location, Dative case is used. 'im' is the contraction of 'in' and 'dem'
explanation_tr: Stock' erildir. 'wohnen' fiili yer bildirdiği için Dativ kullanılır. 'im', 'in' edatının 'dem' ile birleşmiş hâlidir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Wohnung"?
explanation_en: Nouns ending in '-ung' are feminine (die) — 'eine' and the adjective 'schöne' show this feminine gender
explanation_tr: -ung' ile biten isimler dişildir (die) — 'eine' ve 'schöne' sıfatındaki '-e' bu dişil cinsiyeti tanımlar

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "deine"?
explanation_en: Because 'Wohnung' is a feminine (die) noun, the possessive pronoun also becomes feminine and takes an -e ending
explanation_tr: Wohnung' dişil (die) bir isim olduğu için, iyelik zamiri de dişil hâle gelir ve sonuna -e takısı alır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
option_2_en: It is in the request mood
explanation_en: It is a separable verb and in the imperative mood. The prefixes ‘an-’, ‘auf-’, ‘ab-’, ‘ein-’, ‘mit-’, and ‘zu-’ are separable prefixes
explanation_tr: Ayrılabilen bir fiildir ve emir kipindedir. 'an-', 'auf-', 'ab-', 'ein-', 'mit-', 'zu-' ayrılabilen ön eklerdir

sentence_de: Er kauft eine Lampe für das Schlafzimmer
translation_en: He buys a lamp for the bedroom.
translation_tr: Yatak odası için eski bir lamba alıyor
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Schlafzimmer"?
explanation_en: The word 'Schlafzimmer' is neuter because of 'Zimmer'; in compound words, the gender is determined by the last word
explanation_tr: ‘Schlafzimmer’ kelimesi ‘Zimmer’ nedeniyle nötrdür; birleşik kelimelerde cinsiyeti sondaki kelime belirler

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The prefix 'ver-' in 'verbraucht' is inseparable. Common inseparable prefixes are: be-, er-, ge-, ver-, zer-, ent-, emp-, miss-
explanation_tr: Fiilin ön eki 'ver-' ayrılamaz bir ön ektir. En yaygın ayrılamaz ön ekler: be-, er-, ge-, ver-, zer-, ent-, emp-, miss-

translation_en: We would like to buy a car
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Auto"?
explanation_en: Because 'kaufen' takes a direct object, 'Auto' is in accusative — 'ein' stays as 'ein' for neuter nouns in accusative case
explanation_tr: kaufen' fiili nesne aldığı için 'Auto' Akuzatiftir (Neyi) — Akuzatif hâlde nötr artikel (‘ein’) değişmez

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Schlüssel"?
explanation_en: The article 'der' shows that 'Schlüssel' is masculine and in the nominative case
explanation_tr: der' artikeli 'Schlüssel'in eril ve yalın hâlde olduğunu gösterir

sentence_de: Am besten kauft man die Bücher
translation_en: The best thing to do is buy the books.
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "am besten"?
explanation_en: am besten' does not conjugate with the subject and its form remains fixed
explanation_tr: am besten' özneye göre çekimlenmez, formu her zaman sabittir

question_en: Which is correct for the verb of the sentence?
question_tr: Cümlenin fiili için aşağıdakilerden hangisi doğrudur?
explanation_en: Verbs showing movement to a new place, like 'kommen', use 'sein' (bin) for past actions
explanation_tr: Bir yerden bir yere gitme veya gelme gibi hareket fiilleri, geçmiş zamanda 'sein' (bin) ile oluşturur

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
question_tr: Cümlenin fiili için aşağıdakilerden hangisi doğrudur?
explanation_en: Because 'Das Haus' is a singular subject, the third-person singular form 'hat' is used. For plural subjects, 'haben' is used
explanation_tr: "Das Haus" tekil bir özne olduğu için "haben" fiilinin üçüncü tekil şahıs çekimi olan "hat" kullanılır. Çoğul özneler için "haben" kullanılır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "gehen"?
explanation_en: Modal verbs (wollen) are conjugated in the second position. The main verb (gehen) goes to the end in its infinitive form
explanation_tr: Modal fiiller (wollen) ikinci sırada özneye göre çekimlenir. Cümlenin asıl anlamını taşıyan ana fiil ise mastar hâlinde cümlenin sonuna gelir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
question_tr: Cümlenin fiili için aşağıdakilerden hangisi doğrudur?
explanation_en: The past tense follows the "hat...ge-" pattern; the verb "gemietet" uses the auxiliary "haben"
explanation_tr: Geçmiş zaman yapısı "hat...ge-" kalıbıdır; "gemietet" fiili "haben" yardımcı fiiliyle kullanılır

sentence_de: Er hat den Schlüssel nicht gefunden
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'hat gefunden' uses 'haben' with Partizip II, it is the form of the Perfekt (past) tense
explanation_tr: hat gefunden' fiili, 'haben' yardımcı fiili ve Partizip II ile kurulur — bu yapı Perfekt (geçmiş) zamanı gösterir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Schlüssel"?
explanation_en: 'Schlüssel'' is a masculine noun and ''brauchen'' requires an object in the accusative case, so the indefinite article becomes ''einen''
explanation_tr: 'Schlüssel'' erildir ve ''brauchen'' fiili akuzatif hâlde bir nesne gerektirir, bu yüzden artikel ''einen'' olur

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
question_tr: Cümlenin fiili için aşağıdakilerden hangisi doğrudur?
explanation_en: The verb 'arbeiten' is conjugated for 'er' in the present tense (Präsens), which is third-person singular
explanation_tr: Fiil, 'er' öznesine göre şimdiki zamanda (Präsens) üçüncü tekil şahısa göre çekimlenmiştir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Schülerin"?
explanation_en: Because 'Schülerin' ends with '-in', it is feminine, and it is in nominative case after 'sein' — this is why the adjective 'beste' takes the '-e' ending
explanation_tr: Schülerin' kelimesi '-in' takısı ile bittiği için dişildir ve 'sein' fiilinden sonra geldiği için Nominatif hâldedir, bu yüzden 'beste' sıfatı '-e' takısını almıştır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "bei"?
explanation_en: bei' is a preposition that always makes the noun after it Dativ. 'Firma' is feminine noun, and it gets  'einer' (Dativ for 'eine')
explanation_tr: bei' edatı, kendisinden sonra gelen ismi her zaman Dativ yapar. 'Firma' dişildir ve 'ein' 'einer' olur

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "von"?
explanation_en: Prepositions like 'von', 'mit', and 'aus' always make the noun that follows them Dativ
explanation_tr: von', 'mit' ve 'aus' gibi edatlar kendilerinden sonra gelen ismi her zaman dativ yapar

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: The word 'mein' shows that 'Kollege' is a masculine noun
explanation_tr: Mein' kelimesi, 'Kollege' isminin eril olduğunu gösterir

sentence_de: Er schreibt dem Chef eine E-Mail
translation_en: He writes an email to the boss
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'schreibt' shows an action happening now. It does not use the 'hat... ge-t' pattern for past actions
explanation_tr: schreibt' eylemin şimdi olduğunu gösterir. Geçmiş zaman değil çünkü 'hat... ge-t' kalıbını kullanmıyor

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'suchen' is regular. Its stem 'such-' simply takes the ending '-e' for 'ich' without any vowel change
explanation_tr: Suchen' düzenli bir fiildir. 'Ich' için fiil kökü 'such-' ünlü değişimi olmadan '-e' eki alır

sentence_de: Darf ich hier meinen Computer benutzen?
translation_en: May I use my computer here?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The modal verb 'Darf' is in position 2 and conjugated. The main verb 'benutzen' goes to the end in its infinitive form
explanation_tr: Modal fiil 'Darf' 2. sırada çekimli hâldedir. Asıl fiil 'benutzen' mastar hâlinde cümlenin sonuna gider

translation_en: She doesn't want to work today
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "nicht"?
explanation_en: This 'nicht' negates the verb 'arbeiten'. 'Nicht' negates actions, adjectives, and adverbs. 'Kein' negates nouns
explanation_tr: Buradaki 'nicht', 'arbeiten' fiilini olumsuz yapar. 'Nicht' fiilleri, sıfatları ve zarfları olumsuz yapar. 'Kein' ise isimleri olumsuz yapar

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: The capital 'S' in 'Sie' means 'you (formal)'. It uses the same verb form as 'wir' (we) or 'sie' (they)
explanation_tr: Büyük harfle yazılan 'Sie', resmi olarak 'siz' demektir. 'Wir' (biz) veya 'sie' (onlar) ile aynı fiil çekimini kullanır

sentence_de: Der Termin ist um zehn Uhr
translation_en: The appointment is at ten o'clock
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "um"?
option_2_en: Used for all the time expressions
explanation_en: um' is used for hours, 'am' for days, 'im' for months, seasons, and years
explanation_tr: um' saatlerde, 'am' günlerde, 'im' aylar, mevsimler, yıllarda kullanılır

sentence_de: Bitte rufen Sie mich morgen an!
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: anrufen' is a separable verb. The prefix 'an' moves to the very end of the sentence, while 'rufen' stays in the second position
explanation_tr: anrufen' ayrılabilir bir fiildir. 'an' öneki cümlenin sonuna giderken, 'rufen' ikinci pozisyonda kalır

sentence_de: Ich habe gestern acht Stunden gearbeitet
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The 'habe...ge-t' pattern shows an action that happened in the past. Many verbs use 'haben' for this pattern
explanation_tr: habe...ge-t' kalıbı geçmişte olan bir eylemi gösterir. Birçok fiil bu kalıp için 'haben' kullanır

sentence_de: Wo kann er eine Apotheke finden?
translation_en: Where can he find a pharmacy?
question_en: Which statement about "Apotheke" is correct?
explanation_en: "Apotheke" is feminine (die Apotheke). The verb "finden" requires an accusative object
explanation_tr: "Apotheke" dişil bir isimdir (die Apotheke). "finden" fiili akuzatif bir nesne gerektirir

question_en: What case is used in the sentence?
explanation_en: "Gebäude" is a plural noun, "alt" takes the plural ending "-e"
explanation_tr: "Gebäude" çoğul olduğu icin "alt" sıfatı çoğul eki "-e" alır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "ein"?
explanation_en: For words like 'Krankenhaus', the last part decides the gender. 'Haus' is neuter (das Haus), so 'Krankenhaus' is also neuter
explanation_tr: Krankenhaus' gibi birleşik kelimelerde, son kısım olan cinsiyeti belirler. 'Haus' nötr olduğu için (das Haus), 'Krankenhaus' da nötrdür

translation_en: Germany is not a small country
question_en: What is the case of the sentence?
explanation_en: The sentence describes a simple subject and is in the nominative case.
explanation_tr: Cümle, basit bir özneyi açıklayan ve Yalın hâlde olan bir cümledir

sentence_de: Gibt es hier ein öffentliches Postamt?
translation_en: Is there a public post office here?
question_en: Which statement about “Postamt” is correct?
explanation_en: For words like 'Postamt', the gender comes from the last word 'Amt', which is neuter (das Amt)
explanation_tr: Postamt' gibi birleşik kelimelerde cinsiyet, son kelime olan 'Amt'ten gelir ve 'Amt' nötrdür (das Amt)

sentence_de: Wo hat sie dieses Kleid gekauft?
translation_en: Where did she buy this dress?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The "hat... ge-" pattern is the standard way to describe actions that happened in the past
explanation_tr: “Hat … ge-” kalıbı, geçmişte gerçekleşmiş eylemleri anlatmanın standart yoludur

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Apfelsaft"?
explanation_en: "Apfelsaft" is the direct object of "kaufen" and is masculine, so it takes the accusative case "den"
explanation_tr: "Apfelsaft" fiilin doğrudan nesnesi olduğu için akuzatiftir (belirtme hâli) ve eril bir isim olduğu için "den" alır

sentence_de: Wir haben unser Auto im Autohaus verkauft
translation_en: We sold our car at the car dealership
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Autohaus"?
explanation_en: Because 'in' describes a fixed location, the word 'Autohaus' uses the dative case
explanation_tr: in' eylemin yapıldığı yeri (-de, -da) belirttiği için "Autohaus" dativ hâlindedir

sentence_de: Hast du einen Ausweis bei dir
translation_en: Do you have an ID with you?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "bei"?
explanation_en: The preposition 'bei' always requires the dative case. Some other prepositions that take the dative case are 'aus', 'bei', 'von', and 'zu'.
explanation_tr: 'bei' edatı her zaman yönelme hâli (Dativ) gerektirir. 'aus', 'bei', 'von', 'zu' gibi edatlar daima Dativ alır.

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: "war" is the simple past (Präteritum) form of the verb "sein"
explanation_tr: "War" fiili, "sein" (olmak) fiilinin basit geçmiş zaman (Präteritum) çekimidir ve geçmişte tamamlanmış bir durumu ifade eder.

question_en: Who is the subject in this sentence?
explanation_en: German sentence structure does not require the subject to be in the first position, but the verb must always be in the second position; here, the subject is “sein Reisepass”
explanation_tr: Almanca cümle yapısında öznenin birinci sırada olması bir zorunluluk değildir, ancak fiil her zaman ikinci sırada olmalıdır; burada özne "sein Reisepass"tir

sentence_de: Geht sie am Samstagabend ins Kino?
translation_en: Is she going to the cinema on Saturday evening?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Kino"?
explanation_en: The sentence uses the accusative case. "Kino" is a neuter noun, and "ins" is the contraction of "in das"
explanation_tr: Cümle, akuzatif halini kullanmaktadır. "Kino" nötr bir isimdir ve "ins", "in das"ın kısaltmasıdır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Museen"?
explanation_en: “Museen” is plural, and “hat” takes an accusative object; the -e endings in “viele” and “moderne” show the plural form
explanation_tr: “Museen” çoğuldur ve “hat” fiili Akuzatif nesne alır; “viele” ve “moderne” kelimelerindeki -e ekleri çoğul ekidir

translation_en: The restaurant is closed on Wednesdays
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The “ist … ge-” pattern is a past tense form
explanation_tr: “ist … ge-” kalıbı, geçmiş zamandır

translation_en: You need to see a doctor
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "müssen"?
explanation_en: In a sentence with "müssen", the helper verb takes the 2nd spot, and the action verb "gehen" moves to the very end
explanation_tr: "müssen" (zorunda olmak) gibi modal fiiller ikinci sırada çekimlenir. Asıl fiil olan 'gehen' ise cümlenin sonuna yalın hâlde kalır

sentence_de: Ist das der Weg zum Bahnhof?
translation_en: Is this the way to Hamburg?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "zum"?
explanation_en: The particle "zu" always takes the dative case, and when the noun that follows is masculine or neuter, "dem" is used, making "zu dem" "zum"
explanation_tr: "Zu" edatı her zaman datif halini alır ve eğer ondan sonra gelen isim eril veya nötr ise "dem" kullanılır ve "zu dem" "zum" olur

sentence_de: Der Bus nach Berlin fährt an der Ausfahrt 2 ab
translation_en: The bus to Berlin departs from exit 2
translation_tr: Berlin'e giden otobüs 2 numaralı çıkıştan kalkmaktadır
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: In a simple main clause, the conjugated verb always stands in the second position. The subject or another element can be in the first position
explanation_tr: Basit bir cümlede, çekimli fiil her zaman ikinci konumda yer alır. Özne veya başka bir unsur birinci konumda olabilir

sentence_de: Sie lernt seit 5 Monaten in der Schule
translation_en: She has been attending school for 5 months
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: The word “sie” could mean either “she” or “they,” but from the verb’s conjugation (lernt) we can tell that here it is singular
explanation_tr: “Sie” kelimesi “o” (dişil) veya “onlar” anlamına gelebilir, ancak fiilin çekiminden (lernt) burada tekil olduğunu anlayabiliriz

sentence_de: Wo können wir ein Restaurant suchen?
translation_en: Where can we look for a restaurant?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "können"?
explanation_en: Modal verbs are conjugated in the second position, and the main verb (suchen) goes to the end in its infinitive form
explanation_tr: Modal fiiller çekimli hâlde 2. sırada yer alır, asıl fiil (suchen) ise mastar olarak cümlenin sonuna gider

sentence_de: Ich habe ein Kilo Birnen gekauft
translation_en: I bought a kilo of pears
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The 'habe...ge-t' pattern is used with 'haben' and a past participle to describe actions in the past
explanation_tr: habe...ge-t' yapısı, 'haben' yardımcı fiili ve geçmiş zaman ortacı ile geçmişte tamamlanmış eylemleri anlatır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "meinen"?
explanation_en: The verb 'trinken' needs Akkusative. Masculine nouns change 'mein' to 'meinen' in accusative case
explanation_tr: trinken' fiili Belirtme hâli (Neyi?) ister. Eril isimlerde 'mein' kelimesi 'meinen' olur

question_en: Which is correct for the verb of the sentence?
question_tr: Cümlenin fiili için aşağıdakilerden hangisi doğrudur?
explanation_en: The 'haben... ge-t' pattern shows a past action; 'gekauft' is not conjugated according to the subject
explanation_tr: haben... ge-t' kalıbı geçmişteki bir eylemi gösterir; 'gekauft' özneye göre çekimlenmez, hep aynı kalır

question_en: Which is correct for the verb of the sentence?
question_tr: Cümlenin fiili için aşağıdakilerden hangisi doğrudur?
explanation_en: The 'hast... ge-t' pattern shows a past action. The 'gekocht' part is fixed and does not change for 'du' or other subjects
explanation_tr: hast... ge-t' kalıbı geçmiş bir eylemi gösterir. 'gekocht' kısmı sabittir ve 'du' veya başka özne için değişmez

sentence_de: Holst du heute das Brot ab?
translation_en: Are you picking up the bread today?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
question_tr: Cümlenin fiili için aşağıdakilerden hangisi doğrudur?
explanation_en: Abholen' is a separable verb. 'Holst' is conjugated for 'du' and placed second, with 'ab' at the end of the sentence
explanation_tr: Abholen' ayrılabilir bir fiildir. 'du' öznesine göre çekimlenir ve 'holst' cümlenin ikinci sırasında, 'ab' ise sonunda yer alır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for 'Kaffee'?
explanation_en: The verb 'machen' requires the accusative case, and 'Kaffee' is a masculine noun, so 'ein' changes to 'einen'
explanation_tr: machen' fiili Akuzatif (belirtme -i) hâli gerektirir ve 'Kaffee' eril bir isimdir, bu yüzden 'ein' 'einen' olur

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Welches'?
explanation_en: Because 'mag' requires the accusative case and 'Getränk' is a neuter noun (das), 'Welche' is used with '-s' ending
explanation_tr: Getränk' nötr (das) bir isimdir ve 'mag' fiili belirtme hâli (Akuzatif) gerektirir; bu nedenle 'Welche' sonuna '-s' alır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: The verb 'hat' is conjugated for a singular subject, indicating 'Sie' refers to 'she', which is third-person singular
explanation_tr: Fiil 'hat', tekil bir özne için çekimlenmiştir, bu da 'Sie'nin 'o' (kadın) anlamına geldiğini ve üçüncü tekil şahıs olduğunu gösterir

sentence_de: Er mag Brot mit Honig und Marmelade
translation_en: He likes bread with honey and jam
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'mag' is used with 'er' (he), 'sie' (she), or 'es' (it) when talking about singular person
explanation_tr: Fiil 'mag', 'er' (o erkek), 'sie' (o kadın) veya 'es' (o cansız) ile tekil kişiden bahsederken kullanılır

sentence_de: Wir essen oft Reis zum Abendessen
translation_en: We often eat rice for dinner
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "zum"?
explanation_en: zum' is a short way to say 'zu dem'. The word 'zu' always uses the Dative case
explanation_tr: zum', 'zu dem' kelimelerinin kısaltılmış hâlidir. 'zu' edatı her zaman Yönelme hâlini (Dativ) kullanır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Banane"?
explanation_en: Many nouns ending in -e are feminine, like "Banane". This means its definite article is "die" and its indefinite article is "eine"
explanation_tr: -e ile biten birçok isim dişildir. Bu yüzden belirli artikeli "die", belirsiz artikeli ise "eine" olur

sentence_de: Er hatte Brot und Käse zum Frühstück
translation_en: He had bread and cheese for breakfast
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: hatte' is the past form of 'haben'
explanation_tr: hatte', 'haben' fiilinin geçmiş zaman hâlidir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "keine"?
explanation_en: kein' replaces 'ein' to make a noun negative. 'kein' is used with '-e' ending for feminine nouns
explanation_tr: kein', 'ein' yerine gelerek isimleri olumsuz yapar. Dişil isimler için 'kein' sonuna '-e' alarak 'keine' olur

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'essen' changes its vowel from 'e' to 'i' for 'du' and 'er/sie/es' forms. This makes 'isst' an irregular verb
explanation_tr: essen' fiili, 'du' ve 'er/sie/es' için ünlüsünü 'e'den 'i'ye değiştirir. Bu yüzden 'isst' düzensiz bir fiildir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "keine"?
explanation_en: keine' negates the noun 'Nudeln'. The 'kein' pattern is used for nouns. 'nicht' negates verbs or adjectives
explanation_tr: keine' 'Nudeln' ismini olumsuz yapar. 'kein' kalıbı isimler için kullanılır. Fiilleri veya sıfatları 'nicht' olumsuz yapar

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Tomaten"?
explanation_en: The number 'drei' shows there is more than one 'Tomate', making 'Tomaten' a plural noun by adding the '-n' ending
explanation_tr: drei' sayısı, birden fazla 'Tomate' olduğunu gösterir; bu nedenle '-n' eki eklenerek 'Tomaten' çoğu hâle getirilir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The 'hat... gekauft' pattern shows a past action. 'hat' is the helper verb, and 'gekauft' is the action part
explanation_tr: Hat... gekauft' kalıbı, geçmişte yapılan bir eylemi gösterir. 'hat' yardımcı fiil, 'gekauft' ise eylemin kendisidir

sentence_de: Wir brauchen viel Brot und Butter
translation_en: We need a lot of bread and butter
translation_tr: Bize bolca ekmek ve tereyağı lazım
question_en: Which statement about "Brot" is correct?
explanation_en: Brot' is neuter. In feminine words, 'viel' takes the '-e' ending
explanation_tr: Brot' nötrdür. Dişil kelimelerde 'viel' sonuna '-e' alır

question_en: What is the case of the sentence?
explanation_en: Because 'Joghurt' is the direct object (what you take), it uses the Akkusativ case. 'ein' changes to 'einen' for masculine nouns
explanation_tr: Joghurt' nesne olduğu için (neyi alıyorsunuz?) akuzatif hâlindedir. 'ein' eril isimler için 'einen' olarak değişir

sentence_de: Im Sommer isst er sehr gerne Eis
translation_en: He loves eating ice cream in the summer
question_en: Who is the subject in this sentence?
explanation_en: It doesn’t matter how a sentence starts, but who is doing the action. The verb always comes second, and the subject can move around
explanation_tr: Cümleye ne ile başladığı değil, kimin eylemi gerçekleştirdiği önemlidir. Fiil her zaman ikinci pozisyondadır ve özne yer değiştirebilir.

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: The capitalized 'Sie' with the verb 'mögen' shows you are speaking to someone formally. This 'Sie' means 'you'
explanation_tr: Büyük harfle yazılan 'Sie' ve 'mögen' fiilinin çekimi, birine resmi bir şekilde hitap ettiğinizi gösterir. Bu 'Sie', 'siz' demektir

translation_en: We get a bottle of mineral water
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Mineralwasser"?
explanation_en: The last word, 'Wasser', decides the gender of 'Mineralwasser'. Because 'das Wasser' is neuter, 'Mineralwasser' is also neuter
explanation_tr: Mineralwasser' kelimesinde son kelime olan 'Wasser' cinsiyeti belirler. 'das Wasser' nötr olduğu için 'Mineralwasser' da nötrdür

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: Ihr' shows who needs the spoon. It is the plural form for 'you' when talking to more than one person
explanation_tr: Ihr'; birden fazla kişiye 'siz' derken kullanılan çoğul hâldir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Café"?
explanation_en: Since 'café' is neutral (das Café), 'das' remains the same in the accusative case and combines with 'in' to become 'ins'
explanation_tr: Café' nötrdür (das Café), 'das' belirtme hâlinde aynı kalır ve 'in' ile birleşerek 'ins' olur

sentence_de: Hast du dem Kind Schokolade mitgebracht?
translation_en: Did you bring chocolate for the child?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Kind"?
explanation_en: The "dem" before "Kind" shows the person who receives the chocolate. This "who receives" pattern uses the dative case
explanation_tr: “dem”, çikolatayı alan kişinin çocuk olduğunu gösterir ve dativ hâlini belirtir; Türkçede bu anlam “çocuğa” şeklindeki -a/-e hâli ile ifade edilir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Wetter"?
explanation_en: The word 'Wetter' uses 'das' as its article. This pattern shows it is a neuter noun
explanation_tr: Wetter' kelimesi 'das' artikeli ile kullanılır. Bu kalıp, onun nötr bir isim olduğunu gösterir

translation_en: The Pacific Ocean is very large
question_en: What case is used in the sentence?
explanation_en: Sentences that describe the subject use the nominative case. Here, ist (sein) is the verb
explanation_tr: Özne hakkında bilgi veren cümleler nominatif hâl kullanır. Burada fiil ist (sein) dir

translation_en: I'm camping in the national park
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Nationalpark"?
explanation_en: The word 'im' is a short form of 'in dem'. 'dem' comes from 'der', which shows 'Nationalpark' is a masculine noun
explanation_tr: im' kelimesi 'in dem'in kısa hâlidir. 'dem', 'der'in dativ hâlidir ve bu da 'Nationalpark'ın eril bir isim olduğunu gösterir

translation_en: She is sitting in the garden
question_en: Which statement about “im” is correct?
explanation_en: When "in" refeer to a place, becomes "dem Garten". "in" and "dem" then combine to "im"
explanation_tr: Dativ hali Türkçede; yönelme (-e), bulunma (-de) ve ayrılma (-den) hâllerine karşılık gelir. 'in' Bulunma hâli gerektirir, 'der Garten' 'dem Garten' olur. 'in' ve 'dem' birleşerek 'im' olur

sentence_de: Der Bodensee ist ein kleiner See
translation_en: Lake Constance is a small lake
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "See"?
explanation_en: See' is a masculine noun. The word 'ein' and the '-er' ending on 'kleiner' show it is masculine
explanation_tr: See' eril bir isimdir. 'ein' kelimesi ve 'kleiner' üzerindeki '-er' eki eril olduğunu gösterir

sentence_de: Sie haben diese Woche viel Schneefall
translation_en: They have had a lot of snowfall this week
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: The verb 'haben' matches this plural form 'you' in a formal way, for one person or many
explanation_tr: Fiil ‘haben’, çoğul forma uygundur, hem tek bir kişi hem de birden fazla kişi için resmi şekilde kullanılır. Türkçe’deki “Siz” kullanımı gibi

sentence_de: Das Wetter war wunderbar diesen Sommer
translation_en: The weather was wonderful this summer
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Sommer"?
explanation_en: The word 'diesen' before 'Sommer' shows it is masculine. Only 'der' nouns change to 'den'
explanation_tr: Sommer' kelimesinden önce gelen 'diesen' kelimesi onun eril olduğunu gösterir. Sadece 'der' 'den' olabilir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'haben' is irregular. It changes its form for 'du' (hast) and 'er/sie/es' (hat) instead of just adding regular endings
explanation_tr: ‘haben’ fiili düzensizdir. Düzenli ekler almak yerine ‘du’ için 'hast', ‘er/sie/es’ için 'hat' şeklinde değişir

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Katzen"?
explanation_en: The word 'Katzen' shows many cats. It uses the plural ending -n, which is common for feminine nouns
explanation_tr: "Katze" kelimesi, sonuna "-n" takısı alarak "Katzen" olur ve çoğul yapılır; bu, dişil kelimeler için en yaygın çoğul yapma yöntemidir

question_en: Who is the subject in this sentence?
explanation_en: When a time like 'Gestern Abend' is first, the subject 'es' then comes after the verb. The verb is always in second position
explanation_tr: Gestern Abend' gibi bir zaman ifadesi başta olduğunda, 'es' öznesi fiilden sonra gelir. Fiil her zaman 2. pozisyondadır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "bei"?
explanation_en: bei' is one of the prepositions that always needs the Dativ case. Others in this group include 'mit, zu, nach, von, and aus'
explanation_tr: bei', her zaman Dativ hâl gerektiren edatlardan biridir. 'mit, zu, bei, nach, von, aus' bu gruba ait diğer edatlardır

sentence_de: Das Wetter ist sonnig und windig
translation_en: The weather is sunny and windy
question_en: What case is used in the sentence?
explanation_en: The verb 'ist' (to be) always uses the Nominative case
explanation_tr: ist' (olmak) fiili, her zaman Yalın hâli (Nominatif) kullanır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Wald"?
explanation_en: The article 'ein' and the adjective ending '-er' in 'ruhiger'  show that 'Wald' is masculine
explanation_tr: Belirsiz artikel 'ein' ve sıfatın '-er' eki (ruhiger), 'Wald' kelimesinin eril olduğunu gösterir

sentence_de: Es war nicht so kalt im Winter
translation_en: It wasn't that cold in winter
translation_tr: Kışın o kadar da soğuk değildi
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Winter"?
explanation_en: Because 'im' (in dem) shows a location, 'Winter' is in the Dative case
explanation_tr: im' (in dem) bir yer belirttiği için 'Winter' dativ hâldedir

translation_en: What is the name of the lake?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'heißen' follows the regular pattern. For 'der See' (which is like 'er'), the verb ending is '-t'
explanation_tr: heißen' fiili düzenli çekim kuralına uyar. Özne 'der See' (er) olduğu için fiil '-t' takısı alır

sentence_de: Es hat die ganze Nacht geregnet
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The 'hat... ge-t' pattern shows a past action
explanation_tr: hat... ge-t' kalıbı geçmişte olan bir eylemi gösterir

sentence_de: Grönland ist eine Insel mit viel Eis
translation_en: Greenland is an island with a lot of ice
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Eis"?
explanation_en: The word 'mit' always uses the Dativ case for the noun that follows it
explanation_tr: mit' edatı kendinden sonraki ismi her zaman Dativ yapar

question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Winter"?
explanation_en: The dative case is used to express location, where 'der' becomes 'dem'; therefore, 'im' serves as the shortened form of 'in dem'
explanation_tr: Yer bildirildiği için dative hâl kullanılır ve 'der' artikeli 'dem'e dönüşür; 'im' ise bu dative yapıdaki 'in dem' ifadesinin kısaltılmış hâlidir

translation_en: What does he do on vacation?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Urlaub"?
explanation_en: Urlaub' is masculine (der). In this sentence, it gives the meaning of 'in/at' Therefore, the dative is used. “'im' is actually the shortened form of 'in dem'
explanation_tr: “Urlaub” erildir (der). Bu cümlede -de/-da anlamı verir. Bu yüzden dativ kullanılır. “im” aslında “in dem”in kısaltılmış hâlidir

sentence_de: Der Bus kommt um 10 Uhr an
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "um"?
explanation_en: The word 'um' always comes before a specific clock time (Uhr)
explanation_tr: Saat belirtmek için kullanılan edat 'um'dur

sentence_de: Wir haben ihn im Herbst kennengelernt
translation_en: We met him in the fall
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The 'haben...ge-t' pattern shows a past action. Here, 'haben' and 'kennengelernt' together form the past tense
explanation_tr: haben...ge-t' kalıbı geçmişte olan bir eylemi gösterir. Burada 'haben' ve 'kennengelernt' geçmiş zamanı ifade eder

sentence_de: Im August trinken sie viel Wasser
translation_en: They drink a lot of water in August
question_en: Who is the subject of the sentence?
explanation_en: The subject can change position; here it's in 3rd place. The verb "trinken" ends with "-en," matching "sie"
explanation_tr: Özne yer değiştirebilir; burada 3. pozisyondadır. Fiil "trinken" "-en" ile biter, bu da "sie" (çoğul) ile uyumludur

sentence_de: Der Mai ist ein herrlicher Monat
question_en: What case is used in the sentence?
explanation_en: The verb "ist" (to be) always defines something about the subject, and that’s why it uses the Nominative case
explanation_tr: "ist" (to be) fiili her zaman özneyi tanımlar ve bu yüzden Nominativdir

sentence_de: Am Freitag verschenkt er meine Bücher
translation_en: He's giving away my books on Friday
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "am"?
option_2_en: Used for all the time expressions
explanation_en: The pattern 'am' is used with days. 'um' is for clock times, and 'im' is for months and seasons
explanation_tr: am' günler ile kullanılır. Saatler için 'um', aylar ve mevsimler için 'im' kullanılır

translation_en: She goes to work on Mondays
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: Sie' can mean 'she' (verb ends in -t), 'they' (verb ends in -en), or formal 'you' (capitalized, verb ends in -en), depending on the conjugation used
explanation_tr: “sie”, kullanılan fiil çekimine bağlı olarak “dişil o” (fiil -t ile biter), “onlar” (fiil -en ile biter) veya “siz” (büyük harfle yazılır, fiil -en ile biter) anlamına gelebilir

sentence_de: Wir können am Mittwoch nicht ins Kino gehen
translation_en: We can't go to the cinema on Wednesday
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "am"?
explanation_en: The pattern "am" is used with days (am Mittwoch). "um" is for clock times (um 8 Uhr). "im" is for months and seasons (im Mai, im Sommer)
explanation_tr: “am” kalıbı günler ile kullanılır (am Mittwoch). “um” saatlerde kullanılır (um 8 Uhr). “im” aylar ve mevsimler için kullanılır (im Mai, im Sommer)

sentence_de: Ich gehe am Mittag in den Park
translation_en: I'm going to the park at noon
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Park"?
explanation_en: The article 'den' shows that 'Park' is masculine and in the accusative case. When there is a movement, it becomes accusative case
explanation_tr: den' artikeli, 'Park' kelimesinin eril olduğunu ve akuzatif (ismin -i hali) formunda kullanıldığını gösterir; bir yönelme veya hareket olduğunda akuzatif hâli kullanılır

question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'aufstehen' is separable, meaning the prefix 'auf' goes to the very end of the sentence
explanation_tr: aufstehen' ayrılabilir bir fiildir; bu yapıda 'auf' ön eki cümlenin sonuna taşınır

translation_en: What are you doing next Friday?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "Freitag"?
explanation_en: Days of the weeks are always masculine and use 'der'
explanation_tr: Haftanın günleri her zaman erildir ve 'der' kullanır

sentence_de: Sie hat im Sommer keine Termine
translation_en: She has no appointments in the summer
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "keine"?
explanation_en: Keine' is used because 'Termine' is a noun, meaning 'no appointments'. In contrast, 'nicht' is negating verbs or adjectives
explanation_tr: Termine' bir isim olduğu için ''keine' kullanılır; 'nicht' ise sadece fiilleri veya sıfatları olumsuz yapmak içindir

sentence_de: Um wie viel Uhr hat er angerufen?
question_en: Which of the following is correct for "um"?
explanation_en: um' used for clock times, 'am' is for days, and 'im' is for months or seasons
explanation_tr: um' saat için kullanılır, günler için 'am', aylar veya mevsimler için 'im' kullanılır

sentence_de: Ihr kommt im Sommer sehr spät an
translation_en: You arrive very late in the summer
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the verb?
explanation_en: The verb 'ankommen' splits: 'kommt' stays with 'ihr', and 'an' goes to the end of the sentence. This is the pattern for separable verbs
explanation_tr: ankommen' fiili ayrılır: 'kommt' özneyle kalır, 'an' cümlenin sonuna gider. Ayrılabilir fiillerin kuralı budur

sentence_de: Ihr habt nicht mehr genug Zeit
translation_en: You don't have enough time anymore
question_en: Which of the following is correct for the subject?
explanation_en: "Ihr" is the plural "you" (you all), and it is the subject, so it is the person doing the action
explanation_tr: "Ihr", "siz" anlamına gelen kişi zamiridir. Cümlenin öznesi olduğu için Yalın hâldedir (Nominatif)
